title: Atlas - Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP)
organization_name: Atlas Addiction Treatment Center of Oregon
description: Most intensive outpatient level — 4–6 hours per day, 5 days a week, including individual and group therapy, medication management, recreational therapy, and daily lunch. Typically 14–21 days.
engage: Call 503-939-5923 or email info@atlasaddictiontreatment.com for a free confidential assessment. Walk-ins not accepted — contact first.
content:
Services

  • Individual Therapy: One-on-one sessions with a licensed clinician.
  • Group Therapy: Peer-based recovery groups.
  • Medication Management: Clinical medication oversight when needed.
  • Recreational Therapy: Activity-based therapeutic programming.
  • Drug & Alcohol Screening: Regular testing throughout the program.
  • Daily Lunch Provided.

Who It's For

Individuals needing intensive addiction treatment without 24-hour hospitalization. An initial assessment by a licensed clinician reviews your medical and substance use history to confirm fit.

Program Length

Typically 14 to 21 days.

title: Atlas - Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)
organization_name: Atlas Addiction Treatment Center of Oregon
description: Structured outpatient addiction treatment — 4 days per week over 8–12 weeks, with individual therapy, group sessions, medication management, mindfulness, and coping skills. Flexible scheduling to fit work and family.
engage: Call 503-939-5923 or email info@atlasaddictiontreatment.com for a free confidential assessment.
content:
Services

  • Individual Therapy: Personalized counseling with a licensed clinician.
  • Group Therapy: Peer support and skill-building groups.
  • Medication Management: Ongoing clinical medication support.
  • Mindfulness & Meditation: Integrated stress management techniques.
  • Coping & Relapse Prevention: Practical skills for maintaining sobriety.
  • 12-Step Integration: Curriculum reinforcing 12-step recovery principles.

Who It's For

Individuals transitioning from higher levels of care, or those needing intensive support while maintaining work, school, or family responsibilities. A free assessment confirms eligibility.

Program Length

Typically 8 to 12 weeks, 4 days per week.

title: Atlas - Outpatient Program (OP)
organization_name: Atlas Addiction Treatment Center of Oregon
description: Low-intensity outpatient addiction treatment — 1–3 sessions per week (1–3 hours each) for individuals with stable recovery who need ongoing relapse prevention and peer support while managing daily life.
engage: Call 503-939-5923 or email info@atlasaddictiontreatment.com for a free confidential assessment.
content:
Services

  • Individual Therapy: Regular one-on-one counseling sessions.
  • Group Therapy: Peer connection and accountability groups.
  • Partner Work: Support for strengthening relationships in recovery.
  • Relapse Prevention: Ongoing coping and relapse prevention strategies.
  • Community Support: Peer connections for sustained sobriety.

Who It's For

  • Individuals who have completed intensive treatment and need transitional support
  • Those with a stable living environment and a desire to stop using substances
  • People who can manage daily responsibilities while continuing recovery support

Schedule

1 to 3 sessions per week, each lasting 1 to 3 hours. Flexible scheduling available.

title: Atlas - Co-Occurring Disorders Treatment
organization_name: Atlas Addiction Treatment Center of Oregon
description: Integrated treatment for adults managing both a substance use disorder and a co-occurring mental health condition such as depression, anxiety, PTSD, bipolar disorder, or ADHD — treating both simultaneously for better outcomes.
engage: Call 503-939-5923 or email info@atlasaddictiontreatment.com for a free confidential assessment. Note: this program is for people with an active substance use disorder — mental health only is not accepted.
content:
Services

  • Integrated Assessment: Full evaluation of both substance use and mental health history.
  • Personalized Treatment Planning: Care plans addressing both conditions simultaneously.
  • Small Group Care: Limited group sizes with dedicated counselor relationships.
  • Ongoing Community Support: Post-treatment connections to recovery resources.

Conditions Treated

Depression, anxiety, PTSD, trauma, bipolar disorder, ADHD, and other mental health conditions alongside substance use disorders.

Who It's For

  • Adults with an active substance use disorder AND a co-occurring mental health condition
  • Not available for mental health treatment alone without substance use disorder

How to Enroll

Contact Atlas for a free confidential assessment. The intake process includes a full review of substance use and mental health history.

title: Atlas - DUII Program
organization_name: Atlas Addiction Treatment Center of Oregon
description: 12-week DUII treatment and education program meeting Oregon court requirements for diversion and conviction cases. Includes weekly classes, therapy, urinalysis, and documentation submitted to courts or probation officers. Telehealth available.
engage: Call 503-939-5923 or email info@atlasaddictiontreatment.com to enroll. In-person and telehealth options available. Accepts CareOregon insurance and offers cash-pay discounts.
content:
Services

  • Alcohol & Drug Education Classes: Weekly sessions meeting Oregon DUII curriculum requirements.
  • Group & Individual Therapy: Counseling addressing substance use patterns.
  • Court-Mandated Assessments: Official evaluations for diversion or conviction cases.
  • Urinalysis Testing: Required drug and alcohol screening throughout the program.
  • Court Documentation: Accurate progress reports submitted to courts or probation officers per Oregon DUII regulations.
  • Aftercare Support: Optional post-program groups and counseling.

Who It's For

  • First-time DUII offenders in diversion programs
  • Individuals with prior offenses or repeat DUII charges
  • Anyone required by court to complete DUII education or treatment

Program Length

12 weeks. Monday–Friday 8am–4pm in-person; evening telehealth groups also available.

Insurance & Cost

Accepts CareOregon. Cash-pay discounts available. Contact for pricing.
modes: In Person; Online; By Appointment Only

title: Atlas - Drug & Alcohol Assessment
organization_name: Atlas Addiction Treatment Center of Oregon
description: Comprehensive 1–2 hour substance use evaluation by a licensed clinician, for court orders, employment requirements, or treatment planning. Includes written findings, treatment recommendations, and referrals.
engage: Call 503-939-5923 or email info@atlasaddictiontreatment.com to schedule. Free confidential assessment available.
content:
Services

  • Clinical Interview: In-depth discussion of substance use history, lifestyle, and prior treatment.
  • Standardized Assessment Tools: Validated measures of addiction severity and life impact.
  • Written Report: Detailed findings with treatment recommendations.
  • Treatment Planning: Guidance on next steps and referrals if needed.

Who It's For

  • Individuals required to complete an evaluation by court order
  • Those needing documentation for employment
  • Anyone beginning recovery who wants a professional evaluation
  • People transitioning between care levels

Process

Typically 1 to 2 hours. Contact Atlas to schedule an appointment.

title: Atlas - Substance Abuse Counseling
organization_name: Atlas Addiction Treatment Center of Oregon
description: Standalone weekly individual and group counseling sessions using cognitive-behavioral therapy to address the emotional and psychological roots of substance use, with flexible scheduling and alumni aftercare support.
engage: Call 503-939-5923 or email info@atlasaddictiontreatment.com for a free confidential assessment to get started.
content:
Services

  • Weekly Individual Sessions: One-on-one CBT-based counseling with flexible scheduling.
  • Group Therapy: Peer-based sessions for shared support and accountability.
  • Motivational Strategies: Early sessions identify personal drivers and goals for recovery.
  • Relapse Prevention: Coping skills and strategies for long-term sobriety.
  • Alumni & Aftercare: Ongoing support program after completing counseling.

Who It's For

Individuals with alcohol or drug use issues seeking ongoing counseling outside of a formal program track. Suitable for those who need flexibility or are maintaining sobriety with regular therapeutic support.
